title: A man died after getting stuck at the bottom of an MBTA escalator. Authorities are still investigating.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Steven McCluskey died after falling and becoming trapped at the bottom of a Davis Station escalator on Feb. 27.
Security footage showed the escalator continued running for over 20 minutes before it was stopped by a Red Line employee.
An investigation is ongoing, with no defects found in the escalator; McCluskey died on March 9 after hospitalization.

title: ‘Immature’ and ‘out of control’: How an inside baseball feud is roiling the Boston City Council
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Councilors Erin Murphy and Sharon Durkan are in conflict over blocking late-file measures.
Durkan blocks late-file measures citing open meeting law compliance, while Murphy views them as necessary for council responsiveness.
Both councilors accuse each other of damaging public perception of the council during the current budget season.

title: Belmont hair salon gutted by fire that also destroyed ice cream shop
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A fire in Belmont destroyed Lucy Danger Hair salon and Moozy’s Ice Cream Emporium.
Salon owner Lucy Ankner decided to quit the industry after the fire damage.
Ankner launched a GoFundMe to support displaced stylists Sarah Paul Migliozzi and Theresa Curran.

title: Massport is launching a first-of-its-kind remote terminal for Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Massport is launching a remote terminal service for Logan Airport from Framingham beginning June 1.
Initially, the service is available for JetBlue and Delta passengers, with plans to expand to more airlines.
Tickets cost $9 each way, with service operational from 5:30 a.m. to 4 p.m., offering TSA security in Framingham.

title: These spray decks have opened to deal with the record heat
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston and southern New England experienced record-high temperatures, reaching around 94 degrees by Tuesday afternoon.
Fourteen spray decks in Massachusetts, including in Boston and Cambridge, opened Monday to address the heat wave.
Remaining spray decks will open throughout May and June, with some public swimming pools opening June 20.

title: New City Microcreamery expands to Chestnut Hill near Boston College
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: New City Microcreamery opens in Chestnut Hill, replacing the former White Mountain Creamery at 19 Commonwealth Ave.
The Chestnut Hill store offers exclusive 'Cookie Monster' flavor with sweet cream ice cream, cookie dough, and brownie bites.
New City's Chestnut Hill location operates Monday-Friday 1 p.m. to midnight and Saturday-Sunday noon to midnight.

title: Kentucky man arrested in Mass. gun store robbery
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Aaron Woodrum, 33, was arrested for a break-in at Liberty Ordnance Supply in Norwood, Massachusetts.
Police, with K-9 units, recovered stolen firearms and identified Woodrum using surveillance video.
Woodrum faces multiple charges, including breaking and entering, and is held on $100,000 bail.

title: Canton vocational school teacher on leave following ‘reported allegations’
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A teacher at Blue Hills Regional Technical School in Canton is on leave amid an investigation into allegations.
Superintendent-Director Jill M. Rossetti stated the teacher was placed on leave immediately after learning of the concerns.
Canton police confirmed they are investigating but have not disclosed specifics about the ongoing investigation.

title: More than 10,000 votes cast: This is the best beach town in New England
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Ogunquit, Maine was named New England's top beach town by Boston.com readers, defeating Bar Harbor in the final round.
The contest, featuring 32 towns from five states, amassed over 10,000 votes over a two-week period.
Ogunquit is praised for its scenic beauty, walkability, and vibrant local attractions, including Marginal Way and Ogunquit Playhouse.

title: Police looking for suspect after man struck with beer bottle near Southie bar
author: John Waller
bullets: Boston police seek suspect who hit a man with a beer bottle near Lincoln Tavern & Restaurant on May 9.
Initial altercation began after suspect allegedly made lewd gestures towards victim's girlfriend, escalating to violence.
Suspect described as a white male, 26-30 years old, 5'9"-5'10", with dark hair and a beard.
